why: ev := c.evaluate(now) is computed once before the pass-2 loop over c.State.Leases; reclaimSquatter (called at line 1300) closes leases mid-loop via CloseLease, so a later iteration's ev.Class(other) lookup for a different other lease may be classifying against a funding evaluation that is now stale relative to the leases just closed, potentially misclassifying a later squatter or funded lease.

applyResolution's switch now branches on allocated >= expected using baseGangGPUsForRun vs minRunnableGPUs; confirm every branch correctly discharges leases for a malleable run that grew then had its base cut, since baseGangGPUsForRun excludes Grow leases and could pass or fail the gate in a way that mis-triggers or skips the new closeRunLeases call in the default branch.

reclaimSquatter demotes the victim to RunPhasePending when it falls below minRunnableGPUs, but only closes the one lease passed in and removes pods for the one group label on that lease; if the victim run holds other open leases in different groups, those remain open on a Pending (non-terminal) run, and INV-TERMINAL-PRESENT does not catch Pending runs, so a demoted run could keep charging a budget for leases outside the reclaimed group with no discharge check.

Header comment enumerates four invariants 'considered and REJECTED because each is false in a state this engine legally produces' -- these are load-bearing claims about engine behavior documented in prose only; if a future change makes one of these actually required, nothing forces an update here.

reclaimSquatter's doc comment asserts 'The resolver's eviction path already did all three... This is the same obligation, and it is now discharged the same way' -- an indicative claim of parity between two independent code paths that is not enforced by any shared helper or test comparing the two paths.

Comment states 'A Lease is an immutable fact. Its Spec never changes, its closure never reverts, and its recorded ending never moves' -- an indicative assertion about a property that CheckTransition only partially verifies via four specific switch cases; worth confirming coverage is complete.

reclaimSquatter only calls removePodsForGroups when group != empty string (from lease.Labels[binder.LabelGroupIndex]); if a squatter's lease has an empty group label, pod removal is skipped entirely, leaving the victim's pod running on the reclaimed slots even though its lease was closed -- a new half-plane bug of the same shape the surrounding comment describes fixing for R22.

AwaitingMint is computed as activePods[key] > openActiveLeases[key] using only per-run pod/lease COUNTS, not per-group or per-slot identity; a mid-swap run where counts shift in an unexpected order across the same reconcile pass could evade or falsely trigger the WIDTH-ASSEMBLED gate depending on the relative timing of pod emission vs lease close.

KnownToLedger := anyLease[key] || anyPod[key] is a deliberately loose OR the task explicitly asks to be checked for hiding a real violation in production; a run with only closed leases and no pods is 'known' but the boundary condition for a run that legitimately never touched the ledger yet is worth reviewer adjudication.

The fixture's Run GPU-request width was changed from 4 to 2 specifically so the run's requested width matches its lease-held width and does not trip the new WIDTH-ASSEMBLED invariant; this edits the test's own input to accommodate the oracle rather than independently verifying behavior, worth confirming the test still exercises the original node-failure-closes-a-spare-only-node scenario faithfully.

Same pattern as the sibling test above: TestNodeFailureReturnsTypedSentinelWhenNoLeaseNamesTheNode has its Run width changed from 4 to 1 to align with the single-slot lease, per the same rationale comment; confirm this doesn't mask whether the sentinel/no-lease-found path still behaves identically for a wider run.

defer c.checkInvariants(...) is registered before defer func for metrics.ObserveAdmission a few lines later inside Reconcile; Go defers run LIFO, so ObserveAdmission fires first and checkInvariants (which can panic under test, or log/count in prod) fires second, meaning the admission metric is recorded even when the invariant oracle subsequently flags or panics on that same reconcile.

The not-found early return happens before 'before := c.snapshotWorld()' and the checkInvariants defer are registered a few lines later, so that return path never runs any invariant check at all -- likely benign since no state mutation occurs on that path, but it is a genuine gap in the 'runs on EVERY return' claim made by the comment immediately following it.
